description: Compute gross and net returns — differentiating the manager's gross performance from the investor's return after fees and expenses

fields.name: gross_return
fields.label: Gross return before management and performance fees (decimal)

fields.name: performance_fee_rate
fields.label: Performance fee (% of profit, expressed as decimal)

fields.name: hurdle_rate
fields.label: Performance fee hurdle (decimal); performance fee applies above this

fields.name: high_water_mark
fields.label: High-water mark NAV; performance fee only applies above HWM

gross_definition.gross_return: Return before deducting management fees, performance fees, and fund operating expenses; trading costs ARE typically included (netted)

net_definition.simple_fee_model: Approximation suitable for reporting; exact calculation depends on fee accrual rules

performance_fee_logic.high_water_mark: Performance fee only applies when NAV exceeds prior HWM
performance_fee_logic.catch_up_clauses: Some fee structures include catch-up provisions; flag and defer to fee schedule

gips_requirement.disclosure: GIPS-compliant reporting requires both gross and net return disclosure for composites

appropriate_use.manager_skill: Gross return reflects investment decisions alone
appropriate_use.investor_outcome: Net return reflects what the end investor actually earns

validation.fees_not_exceeding_return: Warn if total fees exceed gross return (negative net return is possible but flag)

errors.message: Gross return is required to derive net return

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compare gross returns from one manager to net returns from another
anti_patterns.why: Comparing gross to net produces a biased comparison; always compare like-for-like (GIPS rule)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not approximate performance fees without regard to hurdle/HWM provisions
anti_patterns.why: Hurdle and HWM clauses can make simple percentage calculations significantly overstate fees

anti_patterns.rule: Do not omit trading costs from gross return calculations in public reporting
anti_patterns.why: Industry convention treats trading costs as part of investment decisions; including or excluding must be disclosed

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply performance fees to a period with negative gross return
anti_patterns.why: Performance fees cannot be negative; clamp at zero

worked_example.scenario: Manager earns 12% gross; 2% management fee; 20% performance fee over 0% hurdle

gips_required_disclosures: Presence and amount of management fees
Whether trading costs are gross or net
